title: Cassia turquinae

It is a part of the tribe Cassieae, a group of plants within the Fabaceae family. This classification places Cassia turquinae within the subfamily Caesalpinioideae. Further classification at the genus level places it in the genus Cassia. The plant's classification at the species level is a specific designation within the genus.

The plant is used to treat various health issues, including fever, digestive problems, and skin conditions. Cassia auriculata, also known as avaram, is a plant species in the legume family native to tropical regions. The plant has been traditionally used in Ayurvedic medicine for its various health benefits, including anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant properties, which help to treat conditions such as skin infections, wounds, and fever. The bioactive constituents of Cassia auriculata include flavonoids, alkaloids, and saponins, which contribute to its medicinal properties. Cassia auriculata is commonly used in herbal preparations such as decoctions, infusions, and ointments, as well as in traditional Indian medicine, including Ayurvedic and Unani formulations.
